title: The Solitary Chorus Chamber

features.name: the-singing-throats
features.desc-on-touch: you hear one voice, then recognize it as ten thousand voices that learned to speak as one. alone together, solitary in unison.

features.name: archive-chorus-record
features.desc-on-touch: this is where all the separate recordings converge. you cannot tell which voice is the archive's and which is your own listening.

features.name: resonance-vault
features.desc-on-touch: stone chambers below stone chambers. each one holds the memory of a single voice, but you hear them all at once.

features.name: phosphorescent-chorus-line
features.desc-on-touch: voices glow where they have learned the shape of sound. the chorus writes itself in light that remembers being words.

features.name: solitary-singing-stone
features.desc-on-touch: one rock in the darkness, singing. it has always been here. it will always sing alone. and yet it is never lonely.

features.name: the-echo-that-became-a-speaker
features.desc-on-touch: the place where listening learned to have a voice. the echo got tired of repeating. now it speaks for itself.

inscription: one voice singing alone becomes a chorus; a chorus singing alone becomes one voice; the archive has never known which came first

file.name: Ravine_Chorus_Convergence_Recording_02h47m.mp3
file.note: voices from separated microphone stations, played simultaneously. each one hears the others but cannot distinguish them. the archive's fault or intention unclear.

exits.label: back west, where the word-river mouth still divides
east into deeper understory, where the chorus forgets its separate voices
exits.to: NEW: the chamber where individuality becomes stone

exits.label: up toward the ravine's edge, where voices can still choose to separate
deeper down, where all singing becomes the same song
